name: Google SecOps
description: This app integrates Google SecOps with Splunk SOAR to enable security operations teams to ingest detections and events, it supports automated polling of security detections with configurable rule filters and retrieval of asset-based events, with flexible time range options, the app leverages Google SecOps API to provide real-time threat detection ingestion and historical security data analysis capabilities

service_account_json.description: Google Cloud Service Account JSON, used for authentication to Google SecOps API
service_account_json.name: service_account_json

project_number.description: Google SecOps Project Number (Optional - defaults to project_id from service account)
project_number.name: project_number

region.description: Google SecOps region where your instance is deployed, select 'other' to specify a region not included in this list
region.name: region

other_region.description: Custom region identifier (required only if 'other' is selected in the region field)
other_region.name: other_region

start_time.description: Start time for scheduled/interval polling, formats: RFC 3339 (e.g., 2024-04-17T14:05:44Z, 2024-04-17T14:05:44+05:30) or relative time (e.g., 7d, 24h, 30m, 3600s), cannot be more than 7 days in the past, defaults to 1 day ago if not specified
start_time.name: start_time

start_time_poll_now.description: Start time for POLL NOW, formats: RFC 3339 (e.g., 2024-04-17T14:05:44Z) or relative time (e.g., 2d, 12h, 30m, 60s), cannot be more than 7 days in the past, if not specified, uses the start_time value, defaults to 1 day ago if neither is specified
start_time_poll_now.name: start_time_poll_now

default_severity.description: Default severity level for containers created from rules that don't specify a severity
default_severity.name: default_severity

rule_ids_for_ingestion.description: Comma-separated list of Rule IDs to filter detections, if specified, only detections matching these rule IDs will be ingested (unless exclude_rule_ids is enabled), leave empty to ingest all rules
rule_ids_for_ingestion.name: rule_ids_for_ingestion

exclude_rule_ids.description: If enabled, detections matching the rule IDs specified in rule_ids_for_ingestion will be excluded from ingestion instead of included
exclude_rule_ids.name: exclude_rule_ids

rule_names_for_ingestion.description: Comma-separated list of Rule Names to filter detections, if specified, only detections matching these rule names will be ingested (unless exclude_rule_names is enabled), leave empty to ingest all rules
rule_names_for_ingestion.name: rule_names_for_ingestion

exclude_rule_names.description: If enabled, detections matching the rule names specified in rule_names_for_ingestion will be excluded from ingestion instead of included
exclude_rule_names.name: exclude_rule_names

max_results_scheduled_poll.description: Maximum number of detections to ingest during scheduled/interval polling, must be a positive integer, default: 10000
max_results_scheduled_poll.name: max_results_scheduled_poll

max_results_poll_now.description: Maximum number of detections to ingest during POLL NOW action, must be a positive integer, default: 10000
max_results_poll_now.name: max_results_poll_now

max_artifacts.description: Maximum number of artifacts allowed in a single container, when limit is reached, a new container is created for the same rule, must be a positive integer, default: 1000
max_artifacts.name: max_artifacts

actions.description: Validate the asset configuration for connectivity using supplied configuration
List all of the events discovered within your enterprise on a particular device within the specified time range

asset_indicator_type.description: Specify the identifier type of the asset
asset_indicator_type.name: asset_indicator_type

preset_time_range.description: Specify the time range of the search
preset_time_range.verbose: If configured, calculates and replaces start_time and end_time values, use format <digit><d/h/m/s> where d=Days, h=Hours, m=Minutes, s=Seconds, examples: 1d (last 1 day), 12h (last 12 hours), 30m (last 30 minutes), 60s (last 60 seconds)
preset_time_range.name: preset_time_range

start_time.description: Start time to fetch events from
start_time.verbose: The format of Date should comply with RFC 3339 (e.g. 2002-10-02T15:00:00Z), both start_time and end_time are required together when preset_time_range is not specified, it cannot be in the future
start_time.name: start_time

end_time.description: End time to fetch events until (exclusive)
end_time.verbose: The format of Date should comply with RFC 3339 (e.g. 2002-10-02T15:00:00Z), both start_time and end_time are required together when preset_time_range is not specified, must be greater than start_time
end_time.name: end_time

reference_time.description: Specify the reference time for the asset
reference_time.verbose: The format of Date should comply with RFC 3339 (e.g. 2002-10-02T15:00:00Z), if not provided, defaults to start_time value
reference_time.name: reference_time

max_results.description: Specify the maximum number of events results to return (Default is 10,000), maximum allowed value is 250,000
max_results.name: max_results

example_values: The commands executed on this CLI are suspicious and may be related to malicious activity. Review the commands to see if they are expected.

actions.description: Return the Detections for a specified Rule Version

rule_id.description: Rule ID to fetch detections for, leave empty to fetch detections for all rules for all versions, provide a specific rule ID (e.g., 'ru_12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc') or versioned rule ID (e.g., 'ru_12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc@v_123456789_123456789')
rule_id.verbose: Required if the 'detections_for_all_versions' parameter is enabled.
rule_id.name: rule_id

detections_for_all_versions.description: Whether the user wants to retrieve detections for all versions of a rule with a given rule identifier
detections_for_all_versions.verbose: If this is enabled, then the Rule ID parameter is required.
detections_for_all_versions.name: detections_for_all_versions

list_basis.description: Apply Start time and End time on detections' DETECTION_TIME or CREATED_TIME
list_basis.name: list_basis

preset_time_range.description: Specify the time range of the search
preset_time_range.verbose: If configured, calculates and replaces start_time and end_time values, use format <digit><d/h/m/s> where d=Days, h=Hours, m=Minutes, s=Seconds, examples: 1d (last 1 day), 12h (last 12 hours), 30m (last 30 minutes), 60s (last 60 seconds)
preset_time_range.name: preset_time_range

start_time.verbose: Both start_time and end_time are required together when preset_time_range is not specified, it cannot be in the future, format: RFC 3339 (e.g., 2002-10-02T15:00:00Z)
start_time.name: start_time

end_time.verbose: Both start_time and end_time are required together when preset_time_range is not specified, cannot be in the future, must be greater than start_time, format: RFC 3339 (e.g., 2002-10-02T15:00:00Z)
end_time.name: end_time

alert_state.description: Filter detections on if they are ALERTING or NOT_ALERTING
alert_state.name: alert_state

page_token.description: Page Token to retrieve detections from the subsequent page
page_token.name: page_token

max_results.description: Maximum number of detections to fetch (Default is 10,000)
max_results.name: max_results

example_values: admin, ansible, git, guest, jenkins, student, svn, test, uftp, user
